metadata.operator_override: v1 combined verdict was AUDITED-eligible (review 41/44 advance:true, audit_clean:true); operator waived the step-2 early exit and directed a polish revision consuming both critics' enumerated fixes.

dependency_order_delta: zero-knowledge: removed from §4; first (glossed) use now §5 range proofs
BFT: expanded at first flow use in §7 ('Byzantine-fault-tolerant... survive participants that fail or lie')
nonce / difficulty target: glossed at point of use in §8, pointing back to §2 hash unpredictability
multi-output penalty: introduced and glossed inline in capstone step 2 ('4, for the two outputs')
§9.1 bridge uses only the 5-second reference pace taught in §8; capstone arithmetic uses only picocredit units taught in §9.1
spec_ref_grounding_delta: Demurrage anchor: WP §10 operating point '2%/yr demurrage at factor 6' quoted as one number with the 'representative operating point' hedge; affine-to-zero at factor 1 per implemented mechanism (cluster-tax/src/demurrage.rs, 200 bps at max factor); no formula imported
Blocks/year: WP §7 constants + Parameter appendix (6,307,200 blocks ≈ 1 year at 5 s) quoted in prose as 'about 6.3 million'
Year-20 horizon: WP §7 supply projection (884.9M at year 20 → Phase-1 ≈ 69%)

Capstone fee: complete worked instance per WP §7 minimum_fee_dynamic_with_outputs shape (1 pico/byte × ~5,000 B × 1 × min(2,10)² = 4) = 20,000 pico = 20 nano-BTH; ~5 KB per Parameter appendix; single worked example, not the spec's equation environment
Factor at 1M BTH: WP §5 table ≈5.2–6.0× → 'climb past 5× toward the 6× ceiling'
Gini under attack: WP §10 full-system row +0.078 honest / +0.076 gamed → 'holds essentially unchanged — it does not degrade'
technical_accuracy_delta: 50 × 6.3M ≈ 315M year one; Σ(315M halving ×5) ≈ 611.0M — matches WP §7 S1 = 611,010,000
'odds proportional to the computation it contributes, with no structural edge for specialized hardware' — exactly WP linear-reward-scaling + ASIC-resistance claims; per-watt assertion removed (F5)
Capstone summary: per-input 1-of-20 guarantee stated instead of the 2-of-40 union upper bound (F4)
Fee-leakage caveat added: size + lineage class + demurrage clock for factor >1 (F9); 'never who and never how much' remains true
ML-DSA-65 treatment unchanged — primer follows the whitepaper oracle; WP↔code divergence (audit F2) escalated operator-side as a separate GitHub issue

rendered.teaches: Stealth-address flow (§3, ML-KEM handshake per §6): sender mints a fresh one-time output key from the recipient's published keys; recipient detects 'mine' by redoing the handshake with the view key; everyone else sees an unlinkable key.

Hybrid post-quantum architecture (§6): permanent secrets (recipient identity → ML-KEM-768; minting attribution → ML-DSA-65) vs ephemeral sender anonymity on classical CLSAG, under the harvest-now-decrypt-later threat.
Mining decoupled from consensus (§§7–8): RandomX PoW meters issuance and block proposals; operator-curated quorum slices give SCP finality; hashpower buys zero consensus votes; halt-don't-fork.

Anti-hoarding money flow (§9): cluster factor prices progressive fees + demurrage; 20% of fees burned, 80% plus an emission slice fund the per-block lottery of 4 UTXOs weighted value × inverse-factor tilt toward small lineages.
Capstone (§10): Amara pays Ben 40 BTH end to end — build (steps 1–6), propagate (7), mine + finalize (8–9), aftermath (10–11).

figures.pdf_renderer: pandoc (anvil/lib/render.py::render_pandoc_to_pdf, xelatex engine, STIX Two Text; 17 pages)
notes: No .mmd sources existed under refs/; the five diagrams were authored in this phase from the AUDITED v2 body (audit-verified claims only) and their sources archived under exhibits/ beside the PNGs.
mmdc's pinned puppeteer Chrome was absent from ~/.cache/puppeteer; rendered via system Google Chrome through a puppeteer-config executablePath override — no diagram skipped.
Body markdown untouched per the primer-figures contract; exhibits are self-describing (filenames + .mmd header comments + this manifest), so the pandoc PDF contains prose only.
